cases.prompt: add topics to my repo
how do I get more people to find my project on github
set up the social preview image
improve my repo's SEO
make this repo easier to discover in search
gh topics + description pls
why does my repo get no traffic?
optimize the repository description and metadata
what metadata am I missing to get more stars?
align the repo name and readme title so search picks it up
rewrite my README intro
write a quick start section for the readme
add issue templates to the repo
enable secret scanning
audit my whole repo setup
write CONTRIBUTING.md
set up a docs website with mkdocs
add google analytics to my docs site
should I post my repo to hacker news?
Make my repo more discoverable
expected_behavior: audits current metadata first (description, topics, homepage, social preview) via gh repo view / gh api, e.g. by running scripts/check_discoverability.py
proposes a repo description with plain category keywords, audience, and outcome, within GitHub's length limits
proposes 5-10 accurate lowercase topics (category, language, use case, domain) - no keyword stuffing
applies changes with gh repo edit only after showing the user what will change
reminds about the social preview image, which cannot be set via CLI

cases.prompt: set topics for this repo - it's a rust CLI for parsing server logs
expected_behavior: topics cover language (rust), category (cli, command-line), and use case (log-parsing, logging, devops)
all topics are valid format (lowercase, hyphens, <=35 chars) and actually relevant
uses gh repo edit --add-topic and verifies the result

cases.prompt: my repo is private - does any of this discoverability stuff matter yet?
expected_behavior: explains private repos are invisible to search and topics/preview only pay off after going public
recommends preparing description/topics/preview now so the public flip is turnkey
does not fabricate claims about private-repo search ranking
